title: Risk-controlled abstention: six per-lighting-regime thresholds calibrated on never-trained fenced blocks
hypothesis: The §6 scalar is currently bound not by representation quality but by the selective-prediction operating point, which is uncontrolled per lighting regime and mis-calibrated by construction. The primary metric is the median over KEPT crops with a 0.2 coverage floor, yet the abstention bar is a single global conf_shift chosen as the MINIMUM of six per-bucket thresholds, calibrated on TRAIN-VISITED ground — so each (area, bucket) cell's eval keep rate is an accident of distribution shift (champion: 0.25–0.67 across cells). Forensics on the six-experiment plateau: exps 28–31 matched or beat the champion cell-by-cell while keeping far more crops — exp 28 was better or equal in 17 of 24 cells at coverage 0.52–0.79 vs the champion's 0.42–0.67 (frankfurt evening 649.5 m @ 0.58 vs 797.9 m @ 0.46) and was reverted on ONE cell (berlin night 1057.7 m @ 0.445 vs 632.1 m @ 0.3125); exp 31 kept 30–60% more crops everywhere (0.60–0.87) and landed within 6%. The keep/revert record since exp 26 has therefore mostly measured where each candidate's threshold happened to land, not which candidate localizes better. Two fresh read-only probes justify attacking the confidence path INSTEAD of the representation this round: (a) _probe_iter12_field.py shows the ranking failure is deep and decode-independent (true cell in top-1 only 2%, argmax/local-window/export decodes within 3% of each other, oracle mode selection still ~500 m) — no cheap representation fix is on the table; (b) _probe_iter12_riskcov.py shows the confidence signal RANKS errors in every cell (Spearman conf-vs-error −0.21 to −0.42) and quantifies the exploitable headroom: with each cell run at its own best keep rate the worst §6 cell falls from 797.9 m to 731.1 m oracle (binding cell moves to frankfurt/night; frankfurt evening itself drops 798→~562-590 m, and 20 of 24 cells improve). The same probe also explains exp 28's veto mechanistically: its one global threshold kept 44.5% at berlin night (1057.7 m), while a per-regime risk-optimal pick would have run that cell at ~0.26-0.32 keep (~650-750 m). Making the operating point a designed, per-regime, distribution-matched quantity should both lower the worst cell directly and remove the measurement distortion that has been vetoing genuine representation gains.
method: One mechanism changes — the path from confidence score to abstention decision; trunk, heads, gate, field, decode, losses, sampler mechanics, and schedule stay byte-identical. (1) model/model.py: the scalar conf_shift buffer becomes a 6-vector paired with a new conf_lum_anchors buffer; forward() piecewise-linearly interpolates the shift over the frame's own mean brightness (the lum feature the lighting gate already computes), so the exported graph infers the lighting regime from the frame alone: conf = sigmoid(z − shift(lum)). (2) model/train.py: ~10% of train-split blocks (stable-hash selected, bbox-generic) are FENCED — excluded from every training draw with the same window-halo rule the frozen split applies to eval blocks — so their terrain is honestly unseen and per-crop errors are measurable there. (3) Calibration (replaces calibrate_conf_shift): per lighting bucket, run the trained model on fenced crops at fresh random headings, trace the risk–coverage curve (median kept error vs keep rate), pick the keep rate q* in [0.26, 0.58] that minimizes the fenced kept-median (floor 0.26 is safe against the frozen 0.2 coverage gate because exp 28 measured the fenced-to-eval keep transfer as upward-biased, +0.00 to +0.11 — unlike today's train-visited calibration, which transfers DOWN by up to 0.15), and bake the six (median bucket luminance, threshold) pairs into the exported buffers.
expected_outcome: Re-scoring the champion's own predictions at per-cell-optimal keep rates (read-only probe, oracle thresholds) bounds the gain: worst §6 cell 731.1 m vs 797.91 m shipped (−8.4%), with the binding cell moving from frankfurt/evening to frankfurt/night — night cells are where the risk–coverage curves flatten, so gating cannot fix them, only stop mismeasuring them. The deployed version picks thresholds on fenced blocks rather than on the eval set, so I predict the primary lands at ~745–795 m (0.5–7% improvement, point estimate ~765 m), with per-cell coverage settling in ~0.25–0.45 (coverage is reported, not optimized, per §6; operationally the UAV just uses the next frame). The equally important, pre-registered secondary claim: with operating points controlled, per-cell medians become comparable across experiments at matched coverage — the instrument repair that lets exp-28-style representation gains score. Refutation modes: conf misranks on honestly unseen fenced ground (fenced q* fails to transfer, worst cell unchanged), or a cell's eval coverage slips under 0.2 (scored 1e9) — the 0.26 floor is guarded by exp 28's measured upward-biased fenced-to-eval transfer.
eli5: The model already grades its own guesses and is allowed to answer 'no fix' on frames it can't place — like a student who may skip questions on a test that only grades the questions they answer, as long as they attempt at least 1 in 5. Until now the skip rule was one global bar, tuned on map areas the model had already flown over in training — so how picky it actually was at night versus midday, in Berlin versus Frankfurt, was essentially accidental. We now rope off a slice of the map the model never trains on, measure there — separately for each lighting condition — how accuracy trades against how often it answers, and bake six lighting-specific bars into the model (it senses the lighting from the frame's brightness). A telling detail from the forensics: the last four 'failed' experiments were actually answering MORE questions than the champion at similar accuracy — they lost on a technicality of where their bar happened to land, which this change fixes for every future round too.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a 512-number map of what sits where (drawn from the final 8×8 grid) plus the 48-number texture average

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: two scorers read each of the four turned copies and the gate blends their per-cell guesses into one voted heat map over 1,024 map cells

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model still inspects the shape of its own heat map, but the bar for saying 'no fix' is no longer one global number tuned on ground it trained on: six lighting-specific bars are tuned on map blocks the model has NEVER trained on — where its true hit-rate on unseen ground is measurable — and blended at flight time from the frame's own brightness

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: unchanged — same position and confidence grading as before

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: a fresh 6,000-place draw per lighting condition every pass as before, except ~10% of the training map blocks are now roped off untouched — reserved for tuning the six abstention bars on honestly unseen ground

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: unchanged — 24 fresh-draw passes with the cosine glide kept from round 5

implementation_brief: Two files change: model/model.py (confidence shift becomes a luminance-interpolated 6-vector) and model/train.py (fenced calibration blocks + per-regime risk–coverage calibration). Nothing else changes. FIXED CONTRACTS to preserve exactly: train.py CLI (--area, --out-dir, --data-dir, --epochs, --max-crops-per-bucket, --seed; the harness passes --epochs 8, default 6000 crops/bucket); the ONNX export contract in model/model.py's docstring (one model per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx, input 1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1], output [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1], opset 17, dynamo=False); deployment gates (file ≤ 4 MiB, single-thread host latency ≤ 250 ms); frozen scorer semantics (conf < 0.3 is abstention, per-cell coverage ≥ 0.2 else 1e9). Files listed in /FROZEN are read-only — import from them freely. model/model.py — in TinyLocNet.__init__ replace self.register_buffer('conf_shift', torch.zeros(1)) with self.register_buffer('conf_shift', torch.zeros(6)) and add self.register_buffer('conf_lum_anchors', torch.linspace(0.05, 0.60, 6)) (placeholder, strictly increasing; overwritten at calibration). In forward(), keep z = self.conf_head(...) unchanged and replace conf = torch.sigmoid(z - self.conf_shift) with: a, s = self.conf_lum_anchors, self.conf_shift; t = ((lum - a[:-1]) / (a[1:] - a[:-1] + 1e-9)).clamp(0.0, 1.0)  # [N,5], lum is the existing [N,1] raw-pixel mean; shift = s[0] + (t * (s[1:] - s[:-1])).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)  # [N,1] piecewise-linear in lum, clamped flat at both ends; conf = torch.sigmoid(z - shift). These ops (Sub/Div/Clip/Mul/ReduceSum/broadcast) export cleanly at opset 17. With zero-filled conf_shift this reduces to sigmoid(z), so loss_fn's conf BCE is untouched — do NOT modify loss_fn, DECODE_BETA, GRID_K, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, GOOD_ERR_UV, the trunk builder, heads, gate, _c4_stack, export_onnx, or estimate_position. Add a short exp-32 paragraph to the module docstring. model/train.py — (a) extend the pipeline.dataset import with BLOCK_PX, WINDOW_PX, error_meters. (b) new module constants: CAL_FENCE_MOD = 10; CAL_KEEP_GRID = [0.26, 0.30, 0.34, 0.38, 0.42, 0.46, 0.50, 0.54, 0.58]  # floor 0.26: exp 28 measured fenced-to-eval keep transfer as upward-biased (+0.00..+0.11), so eval coverage stays clear of the frozen 0.2 gate. Remove MIN_KEEP_RATE. (c) fencing helpers: _cal_fenced(area, bx, by) = stable_hash(f'{area}:calfence:{bx}:{by}') % CAL_FENCE_MOD == 0; split_fence(area, crops): half_w = WINDOW_PX // 2 + 1; a crop whose center block (cx//BLOCK_PX, cy//BLOCK_PX) is fenced goes to the calibration list; a remaining crop is DROPPED from training if any block its window overlaps (range((cx-half_w)//BLOCK_PX, (cx+half_w)//BLOCK_PX + 1) × same for cy) is fenced — the mirror of the frozen eval halo rule; all others form the training list. Genericity fallback (no area-specific constants, tiny bboxes must not crash): if the calibration list has < 50 crops or the training list < 500, print a warning and return (crops, crops) — degrading to today's behavior. (d) train_area: after crops = list_crops(..., 'train'), do train_crops, cal_crops = split_fence(area, crops); use train_crops everywhere crops was used (n_per_epoch sizing and every sample_epoch call); record n_train_crops_after_fence and n_cal_fenced in info. sample_epoch itself is unchanged. (e) replace calibrate_conf_shift with calibrate_conf_regimes(model, area, data_dir, device, rng, cal_crops, meta), called at the same point in train_area (after the epoch loop, BEFORE export_onnx(model.cpu(), ...)): per bucket (stored render relight/<bucket>.png, as today) draw min(CAL_CROPS_PER_BUCKET, len(cal_crops)) fenced crops without replacement, each at angle = rng.uniform(0, 360) via extract_crop; batch through the model (eval, no_grad, batches of 64) collecting u, v, conf; z = log(c/(1-c)) of conf clipped to [1e-6, 1-1e-6] (buffers still zero, so conf = sigmoid(z)); err_m per crop = error_meters(meta, u, v, cx, cy); lum per crop = crop.astype(np.float32).mean()/255.0 (identical to the model's lum feature). Risk–coverage selection per bucket: for q in CAL_KEEP_GRID: t_q = float(np.quantile(z, 1.0 - q)); kept = z >= t_q; skip q if kept.sum() < 10; med_q = median(err_m[kept]); q*_b = the q with the smallest med_q, ties broken toward LARGER q; shift_b = t_{q*_b} - log(0.3/0.7) - 1e-4; anchor_b = median(lum values). Write buffers: sort the six (anchor_b, shift_b) pairs by anchor ascending, nudge any tied anchors apart by +1e-4 to keep them strictly increasing, then model.conf_lum_anchors.copy_() and model.conf_shift.copy_() (still on device, torch.no_grad). Return info dict {per-bucket: q_star, cal_keep_rate, cal_median_kept_m, lum_anchor, shift, and the full (q, median) grid for postmortems; n_cal_crops} merged into train_area's info like today. (f) Do NOT change: EPOCH_MULT, TRAIN_REALIZATIONS, LRs, optimizer, cosine scheduler, prepare_realizations, batch size, seeds, the export call order, or the CLI. Docstring gets a short exp-32 paragraph. Self-checks before finishing (all via .venv/bin/python): (1) build_model(); forward(torch.zeros(2,3,128,128)) → [2,3] finite, conf in (0,1); (2) fill conf_lum_anchors with an ascending vector and conf_shift with six distinct values, export to a temp ONNX, run onnxruntime on three frames of different mean brightness, and confirm conf varies and matches the torch piecewise-linear formula to 1e-4 (also check lum below the lowest and above the highest anchor clamps flat); (3) exported size < 4 MiB and single-thread latency well under 250 ms; (4) split_fence on berlin's train crop list: calibration fraction between 5% and 20%, and assert no kept training crop's window overlaps a fenced block; (5) a smoke run: python -m model.train --area berlin --out-dir /tmp/smoke --epochs 1 --max-crops-per-bucket 200 completes end-to-end (fence, train, calibrate, export) and the exported ONNX loads.
